GetProcessHeap

The GetProcessHeap function obtains a handle to the default heap of the title. This handle can then be used in subsequent calls to the HeapAlloc, HeapReAlloc, HeapFree, and HeapSize functions. 

HANDLE GetProcessHeap(VOID);

Parameters

This function has no parameters.

Return Values

If the function succeeds, the return value is a handle to the title's default heap.

If the function fails, the return value is NULL. To get extended error information, call GetLastError.

Remarks

The GetProcessHeap function provides a handle to the default heap, created automatically during initialization. You can use HeapCreate to create additional heaps.

Here is a short example demonstrating the use of GetProcessHeap with the heap functions: 

PVOID pvMem;

// Allocate the memory.
pvMem = HeapAlloc( GetProcessHeap(), 0, dwBytes );

if( pvMem != NULL )
{
    // Use memory here.

    // Free the memory.
    HeapFree( GetProcessHeap(), 0, pvMem );
}

The handle obtained by calling this function should not be used in calls to the HeapDestroy function.

To guard against an access violation, you can use structured exception handling to protect code that writes to or reads from a heap. For more information on structured exception handling with memory accesses, see Reading and Writing and Structured Exception Handling.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Winbase.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xapilib.lib

See Also

Memory Management Functions | HeapAlloc, HeapCreate, HeapDestroy, HeapFree, HeapReAlloc, HeapSize
